objective: Build and validate a closed-loop AI Growth OS system with policy-driven execution, including job ingestion, worker processing, learning feedback, pattern detection, policy generation, policy selection, and policy-based queue routing.
decisions: Use FastAPI for job ingestion endpoint (/job)
Use PostgreSQL for persistent storage (jobs, outcomes_log, learning_insights, policies)
Use Redis with RQ for asynchronous job queueing
Worker executes jobs via RQ worker process
Feedback from execution is pushed to Redis stream (learning:stream)
Learning worker processes Redis stream to generate insights
Policies generated from insights and stored in policies table
Policy matching logic changed from exact match to pattern-based match
Policy condition keys must align with payload keys (input vs pattern fix)
Policy execution abstraction created via apply_policy()
Policy routing must occur at API layer (not worker) to avoid late binding
Executor must remain pure execution engine without routing logic
Queue routing implemented using high_queue, default_queue, low_queue
Redis queue is transient and expected to be empty after consumption
Debug instrumentation added temporarily for policy validation and later removed
built_components: Feedback collector (push to Redis stream learning:stream)
Learning worker (feature extraction and pattern detection)
Retry logic integrated with job execution
Redis-based learning stream and buffer system
inputs: POST /job API request with JSON payload (type, payload)
Redis stream events from feedback collector
Learning worker processing Redis stream data
outputs: Job records inserted into PostgreSQL (jobs table)
Feedback events pushed to Redis stream (learning:stream)
Queue routing to Redis queues (rq:queue:high, default, low)
Updated job status in database (pending → completed/failed)
dependencies: Docker containers (redis, postgres, prometheus, grafana, n8n)
notes: System is operational end-to-end. Policy-driven execution routing is validated with high-priority queue selection at API layer. Worker consumes jobs immediately, causing Redis queue to appear empty. Learning loop, pattern detection, and policy generation are functional. Remaining limitation is absence of multi-policy conflict resolution and scoring optimization.
